name: Grand Marnier Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter a 1-quart souffle dish or casserole; dust with sugar and set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, melt 3 tablespoons butter over medium-high heat; add flour and salt.
4. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth and bubbly; stir in milk all at once.
5. Cook, stirring, until the mixture boils and is smooth and thickened; set aside.
6. In a large mixer bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar at high speed until foamy.
7. Add granulated sugar, 2 tablespoons at a time, beating constantly until the sugar is dissolved and the whites are glossy and form soft peaks.
8. Thoroughly blend in egg yolks, Grand Marnier liqueur, and orange rind into the reserved sauce.
9. Gently, but thoroughly, fold yolk mixture into whites.
10. Carefully pour the batter into the prepared dish; bake in the preheated 350°F oven until the souffle is puffy, delicately browned, and slightly wobbly when the oven rack is gently moved back and forth, about 45 to 55 minutes.
11. Dust with confectioners' sugar and serve immediately with whipped cream.
